claims.claim: Anki added FSRS support in 2023 and enables it by default for new collections.
claims.note: FSRS was added in Anki 23.10 (Oct 2023). 'Default for new collections' is accurate for Anki 24.10+, but EXISTING collections may still use SM-2 unless the user switches. VERIFY exact wording / version before publish — recommend linking to docs.ankiweb.net.

claims.claim: Anki desktop is free and open source; AnkiDroid free; AnkiWeb sync free; AnkiMobile iOS is the only paid piece.
claims.note: Well-established. Article does NOT state the exact AnkiMobile price (intentionally — avoids stale-price risk).

claims.claim: Origa runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and web; iOS is planned but not shipping.
Origa OCR and STT models run locally (offline card creation).
Origa interface languages: English, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean.
WaniKani is radical-based, English interface, paid subscription.
claims.note: No exact price stated (intentional). ADD a link to wanikani.com before publish.

claims.claim: Bunpro is grammar-focused SRS, web-based, English interface.
claims.note: ADD link to bunpro.jp before publish.

claims.claim: Duolingo is gamified and shallow for Japanese.
claims.note: 'Shallow' is an editorial characterization of depth vs dedicated tools, not a factual metric. Defensible but soften if challenged. ADD link.

claims.claim: Migii is a JLPT test simulator (timed practice).

flags: Competitor claims (WaniKani, Bunpro, Duolingo, Migii) lack inline external citations — required by @marketer factcheck pipeline ('minimum 1 NON-README external source'). Add links to official sites before publish.
Anki FSRS 'default for new collections' wording — verify exact version/behavior against docs.ankiweb.net before publish.
gate_reason: Two flags block publication-ready status: (1) missing external citations on competitor claims, (2) FSRS-default wording needs exact-source verification. Both are pre-publish fixes, not content-rewrite issues. Articles are paste-ready DRAFTS for HUMAN GATE review of tone/positioning. Gate moves to READY once citations added + FSRS verified.

brand_voice_check.forbidden_words_in_readme_only: yes (the README lists them as the ban-list — expected)
brand_voice_check.competitor_disparagement: none — every competitor has a 'when it is best' framing
